title: Bachelor of Emergency Medical Technology
content: The Bachelor of Emergency Medical Technology programme aims to prepare skilled, competent, and compassionate Emergency Medical Technologists capable of delivering timely and effective care in pre-hospital and hospital-based emergency settings. The programme provides comprehensive academic knowledge and hands-on clinical training to develop critical thinking, decision-making, and lifesaving skills. Upon completion of the programme, graduates will be proficient in patient assessment, emergency medical procedures, trauma and disaster management, and the operation of emergency and life-support equipment. They will be able to manage medical and traumatic emergencies, ensure patient safety, adhere to ethical and legal standards, and function effectively as part of multidisciplinary healthcare and emergency response teams, while delivering professional and empathetic patient care. Class XII or equivalent exam with minimum 50% marks in Physics, Chemistry and Biology (PCB). The minimum age should be 17 years as on December 31st of each Admission Year. (Medical fitness certificate is mandatory to submit and it is to be endorsed by Government Medical Officer) 01 oundational & Clinical Knowledge: Apply knowledge of biomedical sciences and emergency medical principles to assess and manage patients in pre-hospital and hospital emergency settings. 02 Patient Assessment & Decision-Making: Perform systematic patient assessment, monitoring, and clinical decision-making for medical, surgical, and traumatic emergencies. 03 Emergency & Trauma Care Skills: Deliver timely and effective emergency interventions including basic and advanced life support, trauma care, and stabilization procedures. 04 Pre-Hospital & Ambulance Care: Demonstrate competence in pre-hospital care, ambulance operations, patient transport, and scene safety management. 05 Equipment Handling & Technology Use: Operate, monitor, and maintain emergency and life-support equipment in accordance with safety standards and protocols. 06 Patient Safety, Ethics & Legal Practice: Provide ethical, legal, and patient-centered care while ensuring patient safety, confidentiality, and professional accountability. 07 Infection Control & Quality Assurance: Implement infection prevention measures, quality assurance practices, and risk management strategies in emergency care settings. 08 Communication & Interdisciplinary Teamwork: Communicate effectively with patients, families, and multidisciplinary healthcare teams during emergency and disaster situations. 09 Professionalism, Leadership & Lifelong Learning: Demonstrate professionalism, leadership, adaptability, and commitment to lifelong learning and continuous improvement in emergency medical services. 01 Professional Competence: Graduates will demonstrate proficiency as Emergency Medical Technologists by applying scientific knowledge, clinical skills, and judgment to provide effective pre-hospital and hospital-based emergency care. 02 Patient-Centered & Ethical Care: Graduates will deliver safe, ethical, and compassionate patient care, adhering to legal standards, professional codes of conduct, and patient safety protocols. 03 Lifelong Learning & Research: Graduates will engage in continuous professional development, advanced training, certifications, and research to enhance emergency medical practice and adopt emerging technologies. 04 Leadership, Teamwork & Community Service: Graduates will demonstrate leadership, effective communication, and teamwork skills, contributing to emergency response systems, disaster management, and community health initiatives. Class XII or equivalent exam with minimum 50% marks in Physics, Chemistry and Biology (PCB). The minimum age should be 17 years as on December 31st of each Admission Year. (Medical fitness certificate is mandatory to submit and it is to be endorsed by Government Medical Officer)
